title: Comparison of Structures and Unions
description: A visual comparison showing how structures and unions allocate memory for their members, highlighting the key difference in memory usage.
elements: Member Variables (e.g., int id, char name[20], float salary)
relationships: A Structure allocates separate memory for each member, resulting in a total size equal to the sum of all members (plus padding)
A Union allocates shared memory for all members, resulting in a total size equal to its largest member
